page: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The configuration includes 32 dedicated virtual CPUs (16 physical cores with 2 threads per core) and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2990.99, placing it in the top 25% of tested servers. PassMark CPU encryption and memory scores also rank in the top 25%, at 34148.44 and 2774.08 respectively. Multi-core performance is generally average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 47825.07 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13438.0. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes are average at 76.63 GB/sec and 43.59 GB/sec. For language model tasks, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 286.49 tokens/sec on small 135M models, while prompt processing for larger 70B models is average at 7.03 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the c6a.8xlarge delivers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks by offering dedicated AMD EPYC cores without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. However, the lack of local NVMe SSDs represents a tradeoff for I/O-intensive database workloads that require low-latency local storage. The instance is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and high encryption speeds, as evidenced by top-tier stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks. It operates with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage with a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth. While multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are average, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads by avoiding the premium of local NVMe drives. It is suited for software compilation, cryptographic processing, web serving, and small-to-medium language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, delivering strong single-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for compute-intensive workloads without local storage overhead.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM on an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor
Strong single-core CPU performance and high cryptographic execution speeds
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth across standard benchmarks
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and text generation workloads
